info.name: CarPool API
info.description:
Complete CarPool API collection for Trivazo.

**Setup:**
1. Set `base_url` collection variable (e.g. `http://localhost`)
2. Run **User Login** (`POST /api/v1/auth/login`) → saves `auth_token` (same Bearer for passenger **and** driver APIs)
3. **Driver Register** uses that `auth_token`; response has **no** `token` field — keep using `auth_token`
4. **Driver Login** returns same User Passport `token` → test script saves `auth_token`
5. Admin uses session cookies — set `xsrf_token` + `session` from browser after admin login

**Guards:**
- Passenger + driver app APIs → `auth:api` (User); driver row is resolved by matching `users.phone` to `carpool_drivers.phone`
- Admin endpoints → session (`web` + `admin` middleware)

**Driver JSON contract:**
- **Files (images / license):** cannot be sent inside **raw JSON**. Use **multipart/form-data** on **POST** `/api/v1/carpool/driver/profile` with fields `license_doc`, `vehicle_image`, `profile_image` (same as register). Responses are always JSON and expose public links: `driver.license_doc_url`, `driver.profile_image_url`, `driver.vehicle_image_url`, and `driver.user.image_full_url` for the store user avatar.
- Every `driver` payload from register / login / GET profile / PUT / POST profile includes a nested `user` object (`id`, `name`, `f_name`, `l_name`, `email`, `phone`, `country_code`, `image_full_url`). Use `user` for customer identity; `driver` carries vehicle + license + wallet + the URL fields above.
- **Register 200:** `{ status, message, driver }` — no `token`.
- **Login 200:** `{ status, token, driver }`.
- **GET profile 200:** `{ status, driver }`.
- **PUT profile 200:** JSON-only; `{ status, message, driver }` — `message` is `Profile updated successfully.`
- **POST profile 200:** same URL/handler — use **POST + multipart/form-data** for `license_doc`, `vehicle_image`, `profile_image` (PUT multipart often drops files in PHP).
- **422:** different `phone` than account; `email` must be unique on `users` and `carpool_drivers` (excluding self).

item.name: Vehicle categories (public list)
item.description: Query `active_only` defaults to true (active categories only); use `?active_only=0` for all.

item.name: User Login (get auth_token for passenger APIs)

request.description: ⚠️ Run FIRST for passenger routes and for **Driver Register** (Bearer). Set vars `customer_phone`, `customer_password`. Saves `auth_token` + `customer_phone` from response.

**User Login** first. Category: **`vehicle_category_id`** or **`vehicle_category_name`** (e.g. `Sedan`), one required; **id wins** if both sent. multipart: **license_doc**, **vehicle_image**; optional **profile_image**.

**Response JSON:** `{ "status": true, "message": "...", "driver": { "id", "name", "phone", "country_code", "email", "gender", "license_number", "license_doc_url", "profile_image_url", "vehicle_category_id", "vehicle_type", "vehicle_category": { "id", "name" }, "vehicle_number", "vehicle_model", "vehicle_color", "vehicle_capacity", "vehicle_image_url", "status", "is_verified", "is_online", "rating", "total_completed_rides", "wallet": { "available_balance", "pending_balance", "total_earned", "total_withdrawn" }, "user": { "id", "name", "f_name", "l_name", "email", "phone", "country_code", "image_full_url" } } }` — **no** top-level `token`; keep Bearer `auth_token` from customer login.

exec: console.log('auth_token saved (same as customer Passport token)');

request.description: Login as carpool driver. Driver must have `status=active`. **Response JSON:** `{ "status": true, "token": "<Passport access token — same as customer / LaravelAuthApp>", "driver": { ...same shape as register including nested "user"... } }`. Test script saves `auth_token` from `token`.
No auth required. Find available routes near given coordinates using haversine formula.
No auth required. Returns route detail only when status is open or full.
No auth required. Passenger can view a driver's public profile (name, vehicle, rating) before booking.

item.name: Driver
item.description:
Authorization: `Bearer {{auth_token}}` (customer login token). Driver row matched by `users.phone`.

**Success JSON:** `status`, `driver` (includes `license_doc_url`, `profile_image_url`, `vehicle_image_url`, wallet, vehicle fields, nested `user` with `image_full_url`). **PUT profile** adds `message`. **Upload files** only via **POST multipart** on profile (see that request’s **Example Response** for full JSON with URLs).

request.description: **Response:** `{ "status": true, "driver": { ...vehicle, license URLs, wallet..., "user": { "id", "name", "f_name", "l_name", "email", "phone", "country_code", "image_full_url" } } }`. The nested `user` is the Passport account; identity is aligned with driver row on read.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "John Driver Updated",
  "vehicle_color": "Black",
  "vehicle_type": "SUV",
  "vehicle_model": "Toyota RAV4",
  "vehicle_number": "DHK-5678",
  "vehicle_capacity": 6,
  "fcm_token": "new_fcm_token",
  "is_online": true,
  "license_doc": null,
  "vehicle_image": null,
  "profile_image": null
}

request.description: **PUT** = **raw JSON only** — never **form-data** on PUT (body often empty on server; update does nothing). **Multipart:** use **POST** same URL. Sample `null` image keys are placeholders. **Response:** `status`, `message`, `driver` + `*_url` (see Example Response).

response.name: 200 — example with image URLs
response.body:
{
  "status": true,
  "message": "Profile updated successfully.",
  "driver": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "John Driver Updated",
    "phone": "9876543210",
    "country_code": "+91",
    "email": null,
    "gender": "male",
    "license_number": "DL-001",
    "license_doc_url": "http://localhost/storage/carpool/drivers/docs/a.pdf",
    "profile_image_url": "http://localhost/storage/carpool/drivers/photos/b.jpg",
    "vehicle_category_id": 2,
    "vehicle_type": "SUV",
    "vehicle_category": { "id": 2, "name": "SUV" },
    "vehicle_number": "DHK-5678",
    "vehicle_model": "Toyota RAV4",
    "vehicle_color": "Black",
    "vehicle_capacity": 6,
    "vehicle_image_url": "http://localhost/storage/carpool/drivers/vehicles/c.jpg",
    "status": "active",
    "is_verified": true,
    "is_online": true,
    "rating": 5,
    "total_completed_rides": 3,
    "wallet": { "available_balance": 0, "pending_balance": 0, "total_earned": 0, "total_withdrawn": 0 },
    "user": { "id": 10, "name": "John Driver Updated", "f_name": "John", "l_name": "Driver", "email": null, "phone": "9876543210", "country_code": "+91", "image_full_url": null }
  }
}

request.description: **POST** multipart — file keys: `license_doc`, `vehicle_image`, `profile_image`. Optional text fields same as PUT. **Response JSON** includes `license_doc_url`, `profile_image_url`, `vehicle_image_url`, `user.image_full_url` (see Example Response). Do not set Content-Type manually.

response.name: 200 — JSON after multipart upload (example)
response.body:
{
  "status": true,
  "message": "Profile updated successfully.",
  "driver": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "John Driver",
    "phone": "9876543210",
    "country_code": "+91",
    "email": null,
    "gender": "male",
    "license_number": "DL-001",
    "license_doc_url": "http://localhost/storage/carpool/drivers/docs/new.pdf",
    "profile_image_url": "http://localhost/storage/carpool/drivers/photos/new.jpg",
    "vehicle_category_id": 2,
    "vehicle_type": "SUV",
    "vehicle_category": { "id": 2, "name": "SUV" },
    "vehicle_number": "DHK-5678",
    "vehicle_model": "Toyota RAV4",
    "vehicle_color": "Black",
    "vehicle_capacity": 6,
    "vehicle_image_url": "http://localhost/storage/carpool/drivers/vehicles/new.jpg",
    "status": "active",
    "is_verified": true,
    "is_online": true,
    "rating": 5,

    "wallet": { "available_balance": 0, "pending_balance": 0, "total_earned": 0, "total_withdrawn": 0 },
    "user": { "id": 10, "name": "John Driver", "f_name": "John", "l_name": "Driver", "email": null, "phone": "9876543210", "country_code": "+91", "image_full_url": null }
  }
}

request.description: Driver app calls every ~10s during a ride to push GPS. Passengers polling /track/{id}/location receive this.
Revokes the current Passport access token.
Returns: available_balance, pending_balance, total_earned, total_withdrawn.
Amount >= min_withdrawal (default 10). Must have sufficient available_balance. Auto-saves withdrawal_id.

query.description: Options: pending | approved | rejected | paid | all

body.raw:
{
  "origin_name": "Mirpur 10, Dhaka",
  "origin_lat": 23.8057,
  "origin_lng": 90.3668,
  "destination_name": "Motijheel, Dhaka",
  "destination_lat": 23.7272,
  "destination_lng": 90.4192,
  "waypoints": [
    { "name": "Farmgate", "lat": 23.7567, "lng": 90.3871 }
  ],
  "ride_type": "scheduled",
  "departure_at": "2026-04-15 08:00:00",
  "total_seats": 3,
  "price_per_seat": 80,
  "currency": "BDT",
  "estimated_duration_min": 45,
  "estimated_distance_km": 12.5,
  "note": "No smoking inside the car"
}

request.description: Driver must be is_verified=true. departure_at must be future. total_seats cannot exceed vehicle_capacity. Auto-saves route_id.

query.description: Options: open | full | departed | completed | cancelled | all

request.description: Route must be open or full. Transitions all confirmed bookings to departed.
Fires CarPoolRideCompletedEvent per booking. Settles driver wallet (admin commission deducted). Notifies passengers.

item.name: User (Carpool)

request.description: Customer profile + carpool stats: total_bookings, completed_rides, cancelled_rides, pending_bookings, reviews_given.
Returns current active booking (status: confirmed or departed). Returns null if no active ride.

item.name: Saved Passengers
item.description: Manage a personal list of frequent travelling companions. Use when creating bookings to quickly fill passenger details.

request.description: Returns all saved companions for the logged-in customer. Sorted by name.
Add one or more saved companions at once. gender options: male | female | other. Auto-saves first saved_passenger_id.
Remove a saved companion. Only the owner can delete their own entries.

body.raw:
{
  "route_id": {{route_id}},
  "seat_count": 2,
  "pickup_name": "Mirpur 10 Metro Station",
  "pickup_lat": 23.8057,
  "pickup_lng": 90.3668,
  "drop_name": "Motijheel Bus Stand",
  "drop_lat": 23.7272,
  "drop_lng": 90.4192,
  "payment_method": "wallet",
  "passengers": [
    { "name": "Alice Rahman",  "phone": "01700000002", "gender": "female" },
    { "name": "Bob Hossain",   "phone": "01700000003", "gender": "male" }
  ]
}

request.description: Wallet charged immediately. Booking auto-confirms. Fires CarPoolBookingConfirmedEvent. Auto-saves booking_id.
Returns booking (pending_payment) + transaction. Send to gateway, then call Confirm Payment. Auto-saves booking_id.

query.description: Options: pending_payment | confirmed | departed | completed | cancelled | all

request.description: Call after gateway confirms success. Booking transitions to confirmed. Fires CarPoolBookingConfirmedEvent.
Only pending_payment or confirmed can be cancelled. Auto-refund if already paid. Seats released.
Booking must be completed. One review per booking. rating 1-5. Updates driver avg rating.
Full tracking: booking status, driver location, route details, status history timeline. Only for confirmed/departed/completed.
Lightweight GPS poll. Call every 5-10s while ride is active. Returns lat, lng, updated_at.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Session-based auth. Set xsrf_token and session from browser cookies after logging into admin panel.

query.description: Search by name, phone, email, vehicle_number

request.description: Sets is_verified=true. Required before driver can create routes.
Options: active | inactive | suspended

query.description: Options: open | full | departed | completed | cancelled
Options: pending_payment | confirmed | departed | completed | cancelled

request.description: Returns: total_bookings, total_revenue, total_commission, total_driver_paid.

query.description: Options: pending | approved | rejected | paid | all

request.description: Deducts available_balance, increments total_withdrawn, creates transaction, notifies driver via FCM.
